FIG. 1. Gauge-equivariant GNN architecture. (a) The input consists of matrix-valued gauge linksU jk defined on the lattice
edges. Throughout the network, both node featuresV (ℓ)
i,α and edge featuresE (ℓ)
ij,β are complexN f ×N f matrices (elements
ofC Nf ×Nf ), ensuring the correct transformation under local gauge operations. The model is composed of successive hidden
lattices followed by an output lattice. (b) Initialization: the first-layer vertex featureV (1)
i,α is constructed from ordered products
of nearby gauge linksU jk around elementary plaquettes adjacent to sitei, encoding local gauge-covariant information. (c) Node
update: at layerℓ, the vertex featureV (ℓ)
i,α is updated via gauge-covariant aggregation of neighboring node and edge features,
